message:
feat(alerts): lifecycle states + cleared-retention window for self-healing alerts (bd-3ogaz)

When a circuit-breaker transiently opens and then self-heals (e.g. a
brief network blip), the old alert model left the "Circuit opened
for worker X" warning pinned on `rch status` long after the fleet
returned to 9/9 healthy. New agents seeing `✓ RCH is ready (9/9
workers healthy)` immediately followed by three "[warning] Circuit
opened" lines would over-react and run multiple corrective
`rch workers probe` cycles and daemon reloads before realizing the
alerts were stale. Evidence: css session e125e31f of asupersync.

This introduces a two-state alert lifecycle so the CLI can visually
distinguish still-firing alerts from recently-healed ones, and can
auto-evict the healed ones once they've been visible long enough
for a human to notice them.

### Daemon side (rchd)

`rchd/src/alerts.rs`:
  - New `AlertState` enum: `Active` | `ClearedPendingClean`
  - `Alert` now carries `first_seen`, `last_seen`, `cleared_at`,
    and `state` fields. `first_seen` is stable across duplicate
    re-raises within the same lifecycle; `last_seen` updates on
    every `upsert_alert`; `cleared_at` is populated only when the
    alert transitions to `ClearedPendingClean`.
  - `AlertConfig.cleared_retention` controls how long a cleared
    alert stays in the active list before being dropped entirely.
  - `AlertManager::handle_circuit_closed(worker_id)` transitions
    an Active CircuitOpen alert to ClearedPendingClean, stamping
    `cleared_at`. Subsequent prune cycles evict it once
    `cleared_retention` elapses.

`rchd/src/health.rs`:
  - Wire the existing circuit-breaker state machine so that a
    transition from `CircuitState::Open` to any non-Open state
    calls `alert_mgr.handle_circuit_closed(&worker_id)`. Without
    this, the Active alert never transitions to ClearedPendingClean
    and the retention window is never entered.

`rchd/src/main.rs`:
  - Thread `rch_config.alerts.cleared_retention_secs` through into
    the `AlertConfig` built at daemon startup (clamped to i64 range
    for chrono).

### Shared config (rch-common)

`rch-common/src/types.rs`:
  - `AlertsConfig.cleared_retention_secs: u64` (default 300s via
    `serde(default)` so existing configs parse unchanged).
  - Default constant `default_alert_cleared_retention_secs() = 300`.

### CLI side (rch)

`rch/src/status_types.rs`:
  - `AlertInfoFromApi` gains `first_seen`, `last_seen`,
    `cleared_at: Option<String>`, and `state: String`. All are
    `serde(default)` so older daemons that don't emit them still
    parse; `state` defaults to `"active"`.

`rch/src/status_display.rs`:
  - Cleared-but-retained alerts are rendered with `style.muted()`
    for both the severity chip ("warning (cleared)") and the
    message body, so humans and agents can tell at a glance that
    the condition has already healed and doesn't need action.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -205,6 +205,24 @@ pub struct AlertInfoFromApi {
     #[serde(default)]
     pub worker_id: Option<String>,
     pub created_at: String,
+    /// First time the alert was raised. `serde(default)` so older daemons
+    /// that don't emit this field still parse.
+    #[serde(default)]
+    pub first_seen: String,
+    /// Last time the underlying condition was observed.
+    #[serde(default)]
+    pub last_seen: String,
+    /// Populated only when the alert is in the `cleared_pending_clean` state.
+    #[serde(default)]
+    pub cleared_at: Option<String>,
+    /// Lifecycle state: `active` or `cleared_pending_clean`. Defaults to
+    /// `active` for compatibility with daemons that don't emit this field.
+    #[serde(default = "default_alert_state_active")]
+    pub state: String,
+}
+
+fn default_alert_state_active() -> String {
+    "active".to_string()
 }
 
 /// Build statistics from API.
